channel.title: The Vault — AI Edition
channel.description: Your daily intelligence brief on the world of AI. 15-20 deeply researched articles delivered every morning.

item.title: The Vault - AI Edition: July 10, 2026
item.description: 12 stories: Meta's in-house 'Iris' AI chip and 14GW compute push; funding for Ollama, Prime Intellect and Norm Ai; Rackspace's enterprise-AI pivot; Meta's Muse Spark 1.1 and NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Ultra; OpenAI's ChatGPT Work; KAIST's VOTP and Nature Medicine's clinical-AI studies; newspapers seeking OpenAI sanctions; and Colorado's AI bias law frozen.

item.title: The Vault - AI Edition: July 9, 2026
item.description: 15 stories on the day OpenAI's GPT-5.6 and SpaceXAI's Grok 4.5 launched publicly: model tiers and pricing, Cerebras inference, SpaceX's AI-device denial, Tesla's AI spend cap, CuspAI and Kaon AI funding, Microsoft's Japan buildout, ICML diffusion theory, a DNA-writing chip, EU AI Act changes, and the White House frontier framework.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — July 8, 2026
item.description: 15 stories covering Meta cutting roughly 8,000 jobs as Mark Zuckerberg admits its AI agents stalled for four months, Thrive Holdings raising $2 billion to buy and rewire professional-services firms with AI, Jim Keller denying Qualcomm's reported $10 billion bid for Tenstorrent as the RISC-V chip race heats up, Morgan Stanley pegging Nvidia's Vera Rubin VR200 rack at $7.8 million, Bespoke Labs raising $40 million to build the post-training layer of AI, OpenAI launching GPT-Live, a full-duplex voice model that listens and speaks at once, OpenAI shipping GPT-Realtime-2.1 for low-latency voice agents in the API, an ICML award-winning paper arguing diffusion language models waste their best feature, an ICML-honored paper estimating language models memorize about 3.6 bits per parameter, the A3C paper winning ICML's Test of Time award, an ICML position paper warning the alignment community is building a censor's toolkit, China ordering AI companion apps to disable personalized agents by July 15, Trump abruptly cancelling an AI executive order signing while citing the race with China, Anthropic turning on government-ID verification for its most powerful models, and Alberta becoming the first non-US government to publish a Claude cybersecurity case study. 13,413 words, 59 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — July 7, 2026
item.description: 16 stories covering Anthropic overtaking OpenAI on revenue at a $47B annualized run rate ahead of its IPO, SK Hynix seeking a $28 billion US IPO that brings the AI memory boom to public markets, Syntiant filing for a US IPO on a bet on ultra-low-power edge AI, Anthropic signing a $19 billion data-center lease with TeraWulf to lock in compute, Even Realities raising $150M from Meituan and Tencent as AI wearables find a market, Z.ai launching ZCode as the first open-weight agentic coding environment on GLM-5.2, Claude Code making Manual the default permission mode to put humans back in the loop, Anthropic's Fable 5 shifting to usage-credit billing, Chinese AI models gaining ground with US companies as OpenAI and Anthropic costs surge, Sysdig documenting JADEPUFFER as the first end-to-end autonomous AI ransomware attack, AblationBench testing whether AI agents can plan the experiments that validate AI research, FML-Bench putting AI research agents to the test on real machine-learning science, the EU unveiling an Action Plan for Cybersecurity and AI, the FTC floating a policy statement on AI accuracy that could preempt state AI laws, Illinois signing a landmark AI safety law requiring third-party audits of frontier developers, and a Human Consent Registry letting people declare how AI may use their likeness. 13,888 words, 66 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — July 6, 2026
item.description: 15 stories covering Tesla launching fully driverless robotaxi rides in Miami without a safety monitor, LeapXpert raising $180M to bring AI compliance monitoring to enterprise messaging, 8090 Solutions landing $135M led by Salesforce to build enterprise software with AI agent swarms, Omen AI raising $31M to monitor data center coolant and predict hardware failures, ServiceNow and Accenture launching a forward deployed engineering program to scale agentic AI, Google rolling out Gemini 3.5 Pro with a 2-million-token context window, Profound launching its always-on Aim agent for AI-search optimization, Candidly expanding its AI Intelligence Center with six new financial-guidance agents, Boston Dynamics putting the electric Atlas into production with Google DeepMind's Gemini brain, ResearcherBench testing deep-research AI agents on frontier science questions, NVIDIA's Isaac for Healthcare bringing multi-agent AI into the operating room, the COMPASS method tackling long-horizon coherence in AI agents, the UN opening its first Global Dialogue on AI Governance in Geneva with 169 nations, OpenAI delaying GPT-5.6's public launch after a US government access request, and a federal AI Litigation Task Force suing to strike down state AI laws. 13,748 words, 69 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — July 5, 2026
item.description: 14 stories covering Agility Robotics going public in a $2.5 billion SPAC deal with Churchill Capital Corp XI, Chevron and Microsoft signing a 20-year deal to power a 2.67-gigawatt West Texas AI data center with natural gas, Tesla taping out its AI5 inference chip at TSMC and Samsung for Optimus and robotaxis, SoundHound AI acquiring LivePerson to fuse voice AI with enterprise messaging, Cohere and Aleph Alpha merging into a transatlantic sovereign-AI champion, OpenAI's GeneBench-Pro exposing how far AI still is from real biology research, Moonshot's Kimi K2.7 Code sharpening China's open-weight push into agentic coding, DeepSeek V4 Pro topping the open-weight coding charts, University of Michigan researchers diagnosing a hidden heart condition from a 10-second EKG with AI, an Ames Lab AI called DuctGPT hunting for rare-earth-free magnets, frontier coding agents building an AlphaZero pipeline from scratch, the Five Eyes issuing joint guidance on securing agentic AI in critical infrastructure, Jamf shipping AI Governance for Mac fleets to fight shadow AI, and the Thomson Reuters v. ROSS fair-use appeal landing in the Third Circuit. 11,424 words, 56 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — July 4, 2026
item.description: 14 stories covering global venture funding hitting a record $510 billion in H1 2026 with OpenAI and Anthropic taking 43%, Palantir's Alex Karp calling frontier AI a 'wealth tax' on business, chip-design startup Oxmiq raising $35 million to challenge Nvidia, IQVIA launching IQVIA.ai with 150+ specialized agents to automate clinical trials, Queue raising $12.6 million to deploy fully autonomous pharmacy robots, Grok 4.5 entering private beta at SpaceX and Tesla on a 1.5-trillion-parameter V9 foundation, Meta's 'Watermelon' model reportedly matching GPT-5.5 on an order of magnitude more compute, the anonymous 'Owl Alpha' topping OpenRouter turning out to be Meituan's LongCat-2.0, NVIDIA shipping new physical AI foundation models as partners unveil next-generation robots, NVIDIA's Proteina-Complexa protein-binder model validated across 130+ targets at million-design scale, Anthropic moving to close the loopholes Chinese firms use to access Claude, court-released Pentagon emails revealing the real fight over autonomous weapons and mass surveillance, Anthropic launching a HackerOne bug bounty for Fable 5 cyber jailbreaks, and a look inside the draft framework for scoring AI jailbreak severity. 11,368 words, 56 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — July 3, 2026
item.description: 11 stories covering OpenAI proposing to hand the U.S. government a 5% stake worth $42.6 billion through a sovereign wealth fund, Venice AI becoming a profitable unicorn with a $65 million Series A for private, uncensored AI, TwelveLabs raising $100 million to build video superintelligence, Nvidia offering startups compute in exchange for a share of their revenue, OpenAI launching a Partner Network to scale enterprise deployments, Blackstone committing $30 billion to AI data centers in Japan, Unisound releasing U2, a 266-billion-parameter mixture-of-experts model built for agents, Claude Enterprise adding admin analytics, model entitlements and spend alerts, a high schooler's AI uncovering 1.5 million previously invisible cosmic objects in NASA data, D-I-TASSER fusing AI with physics to beat AlphaFold on complex protein structures, and the White House racing to finalize voluntary standards for frontier AI releases. 10,352 words, 45 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — July 2, 2026
item.description: 16 stories covering Microsoft launching a $2.5 billion Frontier Company to embed its own engineers inside enterprises, Together AI raising $800 million at an $8.3 billion valuation to scale open-model infrastructure, Meta exploring a cloud business to rent out its excess AI compute as its stock jumps 9%, Palantir and Nvidia deepening a sovereign AI deal that plants open-weight Nemotron models inside Palantir's OS, Abu Dhabi's MGX raising $49 billion for one of the largest AI funds ever, Anthropic restoring Claude Fable 5 after a government-triggered shutdown, xAI launching a no-code Voice Agent Builder for Grok-powered phone agents, Cognition unveiling Devin Security Swarm to hunt bugs across whole codebases, NVIDIA's Nemotron TwoTower diffusion model claiming near-lossless quality at 2.4x speed, Google bringing Gemini Spark to the Mac with conversational video generation, a new Remote Labor Index finding the best AI models complete just 16% of real freelance projects, NVIDIA's Generative Pretrained Controllers bringing GPT-style pretraining to robot motor control, scientists building a synthetic cell that grows and divides, the UN launching an AI for Good Global Commission and its first scientific panel report, a California lawsuit accusing gas stations of using AI software to coordinate prices, and Anthropic backtracking on a Claude Code feature that would have tracked users' locations. 15,748 words, 76 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — July 1, 2026
item.description: 16 stories covering California signing a landmark deal to bring Anthropic's Claude to all state agencies at half price in the largest US government AI deployment to date, National Grid betting $1.75 billion on AI data center power through a 35% stake in Joulent, Bloom Energy and Brookfield expanding their AI power partnership fivefold to $25 billion, Canadian defense startup Dominion Dynamics raising a record $100 million Series A, Verkada partnering with Nvidia to push physical AI into buildings and security systems, Google releasing Gemini 3.1 Flash Image and Gemini 3 Pro Image while its flagship 3.5 Pro keeps slipping, OpenAI planning to run GPT-5.6 Sol on Cerebras at 750 tokens a second, xAI's Grok 4.3 arriving on Amazon Bedrock as the cheapest major-lab model on AWS, Anthropic's Claude going generally available in Microsoft Foundry on Nvidia GB300 Blackwell Ultra, Claude Mythos uncovering 'Squidbleed,' a 29-year-old memory leak in the Squid proxy, KAIST's VOTP teaching robots human judgment from a handful of preference videos, Anthropic launching Claude Science for drug discovery and biology, the Five Eyes issuing a rare joint warning that AI-powered cyberattacks are months away, a UN scientific panel warning that unchecked AI progress could create catastrophic risks, the EU and Apple clashing over a Siri AI delay under the Digital Markets Act, and a Bank of England official saying AI trading systems may need 'kill switches.' 15,504 words, 75 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 30, 2026
item.description: 15 stories covering Anthropic launching Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper, more agentic model that rivals Opus, Meituan open-sourcing the 1.6-trillion-parameter LongCat-2.0 trained entirely on Chinese chips, Google's AI coding strike team pivoting to midtraining after losing six researchers in five months, GitHub Copilot's first metered billing cycle closing with bills 10x to 50x higher for power users, Google opening Nano Banana personalized image generation to free Gemini users, Alphabet closing an $84.75 billion equity raise (the largest AI infrastructure financing in corporate history), Berkshire Hathaway anchoring that raise with a $10 billion private placement, Amazon's custom silicon hitting a $20 billion annual run rate, BMW i Ventures launching a $300 million fund for agentic and physical AI, Pocket raising $11 million as AI note-taking hardware finds a real market, RAISE US launching with $500 million to retrain American workers, Meta hiring contractors to pose as minors and probe rival chatbots, Taiwan investigating the alleged diversion of Super Micro AI servers to China, the Supreme Court limiting geofence warrants in a landmark digital-privacy ruling, and researchers unveiling neuromorphic artificial skin that lets robots feel touch and pain in real time. 14,111 words, 70 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 29, 2026
item.description: 15 stories covering South Korea betting $576 billion on AI chips and tapping Samsung and SK Hynix to cement its HBM lead, enterprises ending the era of "tokenmaxxing" in favor of model-routing efficiency, AI startup Lindy moving 100% of its traffic off Claude to DeepSeek and watching its bill collapse, IBM and Google Cloud forging a multi-billion-dollar enterprise AI alliance built on Gemini, Figure AI entering 2026 as humanoid robotics' best-capitalized bet, OpenAI previewing GPT-5.6 as three tiers (Sol, Terra and Luna) under government-gated access, Z.AI's GLM-5.2 pushing open-weight coding to the frontier with a 1M-token context, Moonshot's Kimi K2.6 betting on open-weight multimodal agents, Alibaba's compact Qwen3.5 putting frontier-class reasoning on a single GPU, Anthropic's Economic Index finding 35% of workers expect AI to do most of their job within a year, RoboAlign teaching robot policies to reason at test time, a new benchmark testing whether agents can reason in real time as the world changes, the US partially restoring Anthropic's Mythos 5 for critical-infrastructure defenders, the dawn of the government-gated era of frontier AI, and Colorado's landmark AI Act arriving hobbled by a hold on enforcement. 14,205 words, 73 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 28, 2026
item.description: 14 stories covering Australia's Firmus striking a $30 billion Nvidia deal to deploy 170,000 GPUs in Indonesia, DeepSeek raising $7.4 billion in its first funding round at a $59 billion valuation, Qualcomm acquiring AI software startup Modular for $3.9 billion, Qualcomm signing Meta as a customer for its new Dragonfly data-center chips, xCures raising $46 million to scale AI that maps cancer patients' treatment journeys, China's open-weight models now holding the entire top of the Intelligence Index, MiniMax M3 pairing frontier-level coding with a million-token context in an open-weight release, Nvidia's Nemotron 3 Ultra arriving as a 550-billion-parameter open hybrid Mamba-Transformer, ChatGPT Enterprise gaining deeper memory and a simplified model picker, CVPR 2026 honoring the year's most innovative computer vision research, AI pushing structural biology toward full protein landscapes and routine binder design, a new paper arguing AI agents are fundamentally rewriting what software is, Austria urging the EU to host Anthropic after the US locked foreigners out of its top models, and a German court case against Suno that could reset AI music copyright in Europe. 12,768 words, 57 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 26, 2026
item.description: 14 stories covering OpenAI and Broadcom unveiling "Jalapeño," OpenAI's first custom AI inference chip built in a record nine months, Google taking its first-ever equity stake in a film studio with a $75M A24 DeepMind filmmaking partnership, Taktile raising $110M from Goldman Sachs to automate high-stakes financial decisions, Assort Health's $120M round to scale AI agents across the patient journey, Anthropic projecting its first-ever operating profit of $559M as its run rate hits $47B, Mistral's OCR 4 bringing single-container document intelligence across 170 languages, Sakana AI's Fugu Ultra orchestration model that commands other LLMs, Kuaishou shipping KLING 3.0 and 3.0 Turbo to raise the bar for AI video, NVIDIA's GR00T-H-N1.7 commercial foundation model for surgical robotics, a new interpretable model forecasting scientific breakthroughs from concept-network dynamics, content-adaptive sparse attention cutting long-context compute by 10x or more, Penn physicists building a light-matter particle to speed up AI at lower energy, the US government asking OpenAI to stagger its GPT-5.6 rollout to trusted partners in an unprecedented move, and Anthropic accusing Alibaba of illicitly accessing Claude with 25,000 fake accounts. 11,427 words, 56 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 25, 2026
item.description: 16 stories covering Meta building an AI-powered prediction market app to rival Kalshi and Polymarket, Anthropic in talks with Microsoft to run Claude on custom Maia 200 inference chips, Qualcomm circling Jim Keller's RISC-V startup Tenstorrent in an $8-10B deal, OpenAI launching a self-serve Ads Manager inside ChatGPT, Supabase's $500M raise at a $10.5B valuation as AI agents become its biggest customer, Gemini 2.5 Pro Deep Think resetting the science leaderboard at 82.4% GPQA, Apple's iOS 27 Siri rebuilt as a standalone chatbot powered by Google Gemini, Andrej Karpathy joining Anthropic to use Claude to accelerate its own pre-training, the "Goblin Incident" reward-model glitch shaping GPT-5.6, Stanford's AI Index 2026 charting faster progress and falling trust, new architectures pushing million-token context toward near-32K compute cost, a study showing LLM peer reviewers can be flipped from reject to accept via hidden prompts, four frontier-lab CEOs urging Congress to mandate DNA synthesis screening for bioweapons risk, NYC mandating bias review for every AI tool across 1.1 million students, California courts piloting an undisclosed AI clerk, and the EU AI Act's high-risk deadline wobbling as a Digital Omnibus delay looms. 14,338 words, 69 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 24, 2026
item.description: 16 stories covering Nobel laureate and AlphaFold lead John Jumper leaving Google DeepMind for Anthropic, Google losing two foundational AI researchers in a week as Alphabet stock slides 7.2%, OpenAI acquiring Ona to give Codex persistent long-running cloud sandboxes, a deep look at Reflection AI — the $25B "DeepSeek of the West", AlphaSense's $350M raise at a $7.5B valuation, Google's Diffusion Gemma promising 4x-faster on-device text generation, MiniMax M2.5 delivering near-frontier performance at a fraction of the cost, ByteDance's Seedance 2.0 AI video model amid TikTok pressure, leaked benchmarks showing a Gemini 3.5 Pro reasoning gap as its June deadline looms, the Energy Department's AI-designed "Aires Tide" flight vehicle built under the Genesis Mission, new "latent world models" letting LLM agents plan in compressed state, Anthropic's Mythos model finding vulnerabilities in classified U.S. government systems, Trump's 30-day voluntary model review going active, a court staying Colorado's AI bias law after xAI's challenge, the UK FCA warning AI is moving faster than the law, and the EU AI Act's August high-risk deadline nearing. 13,446 words, 64 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 23, 2026
item.description: 15 stories covering SpaceX's $6.3B Reflection AI compute deal turning Colossus into a commercial AI cloud, SpaceX stock falling 10% as Wall Street questions its compute-as-a-service bet, Anthropic's Fable 5 moving behind a usage-credit paywall as the free window closes, Morgan Stanley's forecast that AI-linked debt will nearly double to $570B in 2026, Suno's $400M Series D at a $5.4B valuation amid label litigation, OpenAI's launch of GPT-5.5-Cyber and the Patch the Planet open-source security initiative, the Fable 5 shutdown accelerating the enterprise shift to open-weight models, Meta's 32B open-weights Code World Model, the Orion-MSP multi-scale sparse attention method for tabular in-context learning, a trust-first neuro-symbolic architecture for verifiable mathematical reasoning, Generalist AI's $400M raise to teach robots complex tasks, Flourish's $500M for brain-inspired AI backed by Bezos, a Munich court ruling Google directly liable for false AI Overview claims, and the UN's AI Environmental Transparency Initiative. 13,330 words, 64 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 22, 2026
item.description: 16 stories covering Transformer co-author Noam Shazeer leaving Google for OpenAI as Lead for Architecture Research, SpaceX's plan to build AI data centers in orbit as terrestrial power runs short, Epic's Unreal Engine 6 making Claude and Gemini core engine pillars, OpenAI and Anthropic both filing confidential IPOs as the AI listing supercycle arrives, Google's Android 17 shipping with Gemini Omni and Lyria 3 music generation built in, OpenRouter's Fusion blending multiple models to rival Fable 5 at half the cost, Claude Code shipping nested subagents for long multi-hour sessions, OpenAI's Record and Replay turning one-off workflows into reusable Codex skills, an Anthropic study of 400,000 sessions finding domain expertise beats coding skill in Claude Code, AI agents resolving open math conjectures with proofs checked line-by-line in Lean 4, Google DeepMind's AI co-mathematician scoring 48% on FrontierMath's hardest tier, KAIST's VOTP teaching AI human judgment from a handful of preference videos, 42 state attorneys general launching a sweeping investigation into OpenAI ahead of its IPO, China unveiling a $295B five-year AI infrastructure plan, the White House executive order that explains the Fable 5 ban, and Congress unveiling the "Great American AI Act." 15,908 words, 68 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 21, 2026
item.description: 16 stories covering Amazon dropping Luca Guadagnino's nearly finished Sam Altman film "Artificial" amid its $50B OpenAI deal, Anthropic signing more than 12 US data center leases exceeding 1 gigawatt as its IPO nears, ChatGPT falling below 50% market share for the first time, a Black Duck study finding 97% of developers use AI coding tools but only a third have governance, GPT-5.6 surfacing inside ChatGPT Pro ahead of a late-June launch, Gemini 3.5 Pro nearing release with a 2M-token context and "Deep Think" mode, DeepSeek's 1.6-trillion-parameter V4 preview trained on Huawei Ascend chips, Grok 4.3 arriving on Amazon Bedrock, the Reuters Institute finding 10% of adults now use AI chatbots for news weekly, the "Probe Before You Edit" method for LLM drug-design agents, "Latent Reward Steering" for inference-time reasoning, a six-agent AbaqusAgent framework automating finite element analysis, FERC's historic show-cause orders to speed AI data centers onto the grid, the failure of Illinois's POWER Act, Dario Amodei's inconclusive meeting with the Trump team over the Mythos export ban, and The Economist's "America's AI Power Grab" cover. 15,800 words, 78 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 20, 2026
item.description: 15 stories covering SpaceX's $60B all-stock deal to acquire Cursor maker Anysphere — the largest acquisition in AI developer-tooling history, OpenAI's $150M Partner Network aiming to certify 300,000 consultants, TCS and Anthropic's global partnership bringing Claude to 600,000+ employees, DXC and Anthropic's alliance to embed Claude in banks, airlines and hospitals, Swiss startup Prem AI's $100M raise for sovereign enterprise AI, Snap's $2,195 SPECS AR glasses shipping with Claude Code and a GPT-5.5-topping spatial benchmark, Loft Orbital running Google's Gemma 3 vision-language model in orbit aboard YAM-9, AgileThinker's reactive-plus-planning approach to real-time reasoning, a new executable-games benchmark for interactive LLM reasoning, the "Agentjacking" attack hijacking AI coding agents via fake Sentry errors, DrugReasoner's interpretable drug-approval predictions, Anthropic's Claude Corps national fellowship, the G7's proposed "trusted partners" framework for restricted US AI models, and FIRST's projection of 66,000 CVEs in 2026 as AI discovery outpaces human patching. 13,306 words, 63 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 19, 2026
item.description: 15 stories covering OpenAI's acquisition of Astral (maker of the uv and ruff Python tools) to supercharge Codex, Baseten nearing a $1.5B raise at up to a $13B valuation as inference demand explodes, the AI layoff wave becoming a "powder keg" as record profits collide with record job cuts, Anthropic opening its Seoul office with Samsung, LG and NAVER deploying Claude, Google's return to smart speakers with Gemini inside, MiniMax M3 topping open-weight coding benchmarks with a million-token context, xAI's broad rollout of Grok Imagine Video 1.5, Anthropic doubling Claude Cowork limits at no charge, OpenAI retiring GPT-5.2 and making GPT-5.5 the default, Cornell's WARP showing a 13-word Reddit comment can hijack AI search, MIT's DAAAM giving robots long-term spatial memory, ScaleSWE mining millions of GitHub pull requests to train small open coding agents, a patched Microsoft Copilot flaw that let one click exfiltrate 2FA codes, 76 cybersecurity experts signing an open letter against the US ban on Anthropic's models, and a report arguing that ban was never really about a jailbreak. 14,533 words, 72 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 18, 2026
item.description: 15 stories covering Midjourney's first hardware product — a full-body ultrasound scanner under its new Midjourney Medical division, JPMorgan cutting off Hong Kong staff from Anthropic's Claude after Goldman Sachs, NeuralTrust's $20M seed for enterprise AI-agent security, ByteDance emerging as Microsoft's biggest AI customer on $1B+ of annual Azure spend, Korean and Taiwanese suppliers reaping the AI hardware boom as US curbs sideline China, Zhipu's open-weights GLM-5.2 topping the intelligence index and undercutting GPT-5.5 on coding, OpenAI's upgraded GPT-Rosalind life-sciences model, Google's Antigravity CLI replacing the Gemini CLI, the "continuous audio thinking" method for audio language models, OpenAI's LifeSciBench benchmark where the best model passes just 36%, Pew finding nearly half of US adults now use AI chatbots, NAVI-Orbital's first in-orbit vision-language model, Gaussian Mixture Attention's linear-time alternative to dot-product attention, SK Telecom's alleged China ties behind Washington's Mythos crackdown, and the G7 Evian summit's voluntary AI deal on youth safety. 14,184 words, 62 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 17, 2026
item.description: 15 stories covering Cognition's $1B raise at a $26B valuation for its Devin coding agent, OpenRouter's $113M CapitalG-led Series B, Supabase's $500M round at $10.5B, Corgi Insurance's $106M Series B1, Microsoft's $1B+ Thailand AI infrastructure pledge, Microsoft's in-house MAI-Thinking-1 model family, Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.8 flagship, NVIDIA's open-source Ising models for quantum computing, OpenAI's Deployment Simulation pre-release testing, Epoch's rebuilt FrontierMath v2 benchmark, the ExpRL on-policy mid-training method, the LoopWM looped-transformer world model, the EU's draft Code of Practice for labelling AI-generated content, Japan's government and megabanks gaining access to Claude Mythos, and OpenAI's Economic Research Exchange. 14,211 words, 70 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 16, 2026
item.description: 14 stories covering Meta CTO Andrew Bosworth's memo admitting an "atrocious job" selling the Applied AI reorganization, Runware's $50M Series A for its Sonic inference engine, Infosys's strategic OpenAI enterprise collaboration, Palantir's AIPCon 10 push into law, construction and insurance with a Google Cloud deal, Defakto's $30.75M raise to secure non-human identities, Satya Nadella's viral "Loopcraft" essay on building AI ecosystems, the splintering open-weight coding-model wars, the DOJ calling xAI "vital" to national security in the NAACP turbine suit, a Stanford commencement walkout over Google's defense AI ties, Colorado's AI discrimination law facing a DOJ and xAI challenge, QPILOTS test-time steering of flow policies, the PhoneHarness mobile-agent harness, relational structural causal models, and Dr-DCI's direct corpus interaction for search agents. 14,315 words, 63 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 15, 2026
item.description: 16 stories covering Salesforce's $3.6B acquisition of Fin to supercharge Agentforce, Anthropic's enterprise venture buying Fractional AI away from OpenAI, Anthropic's revenue hitting $47B as Daniela Amodei makes the IPO case, Goldman Sachs pegging AI's buildout at $7.6 trillion, Accenture's bet on Netomi for agentic customer service, Moonshot's trillion-parameter Kimi K2.7 Code, the 120,000-character leak of Fable 5's system prompt, NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Nano Omni open omni-modal model, the post-recall push toward "hardware sovereignty," the "pack hunt" multi-agent jailbreak, AXIOM's neuro-symbolic verifiable math, the Arbor autonomous-research framework, a BFT-derived multi-model consensus protocol, xAI's lawsuit against California's training-data law, Anthropic and OpenAI as frontier-access gatekeepers, and a papal encyclical on AI ethics. 14,757 words, 72 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 14, 2026
item.description: 15 stories covering Yann LeCun's AMI Labs raising Europe's largest-ever seed round for world models, OpenAI's first media acquisition of TBPN, Rhoda AI's $450M robotics launch, SpaceX renting out $2B a month in AI compute, Amazon's record Canadian-dollar bond for AI capex, Mythos 5 arriving on the Claude Developer Platform, ChatGPT's new Library and dreaming memory for free users, Microsoft's Surface Laptop Ultra with Nvidia's RTX Spark, MiniMax's MaxProof clearing the olympiad gold-medal bar, EnergyMamba's uncertainty-aware forecasting, the DRPO method for smoother RL, new agent-memory frameworks, the U.S. restricting global access to Anthropic's models, states defying Trump on AI rules, and $130B in data-center projects blocked by local backlash. 15,381 words, 69 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 13, 2026
item.description: 15 stories covering Anthropic overtaking OpenAI in U.S. business adoption, EngineAI's confidential Hong Kong IPO, the Anthropic-OpenAI revenue-recognition split, a report on Anthropic blindsiding partners, the IBM-ServiceNow agentic-AI alliance, a leaked GPT-5.6 checkpoint, Alibaba's Qwen 3.7 Plus multimodal model, Claude Code's /fork command, the D-I-TASSER protein-structure model, Google's agent-scaling study, reasoning-model jailbreak research, FutureHouse's Robin discovery agent, CNN's suit against Perplexity, Europe's AI copyright crackdown, and the EU AI Act's August GPAI deadline. 14,398 words, 67 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 12, 2026
item.description: 13 stories covering SpaceX's record Nasdaq IPO, the Magnificent Seven's $2T June slide, Neura Robotics' $1.4B raise, the Anthropic-TCS enterprise pact, the Cadence-NVIDIA sim-to-real partnership, Claude Code's nested sub-agents, OpenAI's Ona acquisition, Visa's ChatGPT payments, Nvidia's Vera chip for China, CVPR's O-Voxel, agent-memory research, New York's AI legislation, and Trump's pulled AI executive order. 13,972 words, 60 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 10, 2026
item.description: 15 stories covering Foxconn's MoMClaw agentic factories, NewLimit's $435M longevity round, the Salesforce-Contentful deal, the NVIDIA-ServiceNow agent push, Google's DiffusionGemma, Claude Fable 5, an MIT fake-news study, Michigan's Prima brain-MRI AI, CVPR's D4RT, China's $295B AI buildout, Anthropic's pause call, and the Pentagon's Claude replacement tests. 12,541 words, 60 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 09, 2026
item.description: 17 stories covering Apple Siri rebuilt on Gemini, the Suno 400M round, Generalist AI physical AGI, Google Gemma 4, Gemini 3.5 Pro timing, TurboQuant compression, the Microsoft repo breach, the US-Japan DOE pact, and the state AI preemption push. 18,612 words, 87 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 04, 2026
item.description: 15 stories covering Canada AI for All strategy, Great American AI Act, Coralogix $200M round, MiniMax M3, GPT-Realtime-2, Nvidia Nemotron 3 Ultra, Microsoft Majorana 2, and more. 13,825 words, 60 min read.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — June 03, 2026
item.description: 15 stories covering Microsoft Scout agent, DeepSeek $7.4B round, Neo4j GraphAware acquisition, OpenAI Codex plugins, Scorsese AI backlash, and more.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition - June 02, 2026
item.description: 15 stories: Microsoft Project Polaris replaces OpenAI in Copilot, OpenAI on AWS Bedrock, GitHub Copilot App, Windows Agent Store, Intel Xeon 6+, seven MAI models, Codex 5M users, Azure Agent Mesh, Trump AI executive order, and more.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition - June 01, 2026
item.description: 10 stories covering AI infrastructure, IPOs, developer tools, and research benchmarks.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — May 31, 2026

item.title: The Vault AI Edition - May 29, 2026
item.description: 16 stories: Opus 4.8 dynamic workflows, ChatGPT ad platform expansion, ByteDance 70B capex, Figure AI robot marathon, Hassabis AGI warning, California AI bills crossover, EU AI Act omnibus deal, and more.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — May 28, 2026
item.description: 15 stories: KPMG Deploys Claude to 276,000 Employees in Largest Big Four AI Alliance, OpenAI Launches DeployCo With $4 Billion to Build Enterprise AI Consulting Empire, and more.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — May 27, 2026
item.description: 15 stories: Cognition AI raises 1B at 26B valuation to scale Devin coding platform, Fireworks AI in talks at 15B valuation as inference market heats up, Kuaishou prepares Kling AI video unit IPO, Dun and Bradstreet rebuilds data graph for AI agents, Tensormesh raises 20M for KV cache inference optimization, Google launches Gemini 3.5 Flash and Omni at I/O 2026, Google Search enters agent era with generative UI, Salesforce ships Agentforce Coworker for CRM, OpenAI tells US it will spend 50B on AI infrastructure in 2026, MassRobotics healthcare robotics showcase, generative AI outperforms humans on creativity tests, Chinese labs ship open-weight coding models in 12-day sprint, China widens AI researcher travel restrictions, Treasury Secretary calls bank chiefs over Mythos cybersecurity risks, and AI regulation becomes core enterprise operating model.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — May 26, 2026
item.description: 15 stories: Micron crosses 1 trillion market cap on AI memory demand, Commure raises 70M at 7B valuation for healthcare AI agents, Anthropic acquires Stainless for 300M-plus, Zendesk commits 100M to AI startups program, Parallel raises 100M Series B for agent web infrastructure, AI Studios launches context-aware TTS with 1000 voices, SubQ ships first subquadratic LLM with 12M token context, Chief AI Officer role surges to 76 percent of organizations, agentic AI replaces chatbot wrappers in enterprise, brain-LLM fMRI alignment study across 112 participants, AI medical robotics paradigm shift in surgery, machine unlearning via manifold representation forgetting, Colorado SB 189 replaces landmark AI act, US pre-release model testing with regulators, and federal vs state AI regulation battle.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — May 25, 2026
item.description: 15 stories: Anthropic closes 30B round at 900B valuation, NextEra-Dominion 67B AI power merger, JPMorgan AI core infrastructure overhaul, Adobe-Canva-CapCut Gemini integration, Anthropic-Microsoft Maia 200 chip, OpenAI Codex Mac control locked down, Google TurboQuant KV cache compression, Pentagon tests OpenAI and Google for defense, enterprise AI adoption challenges in 2026, Connecticut SB5 comprehensive AI law, AI deepfake political ads threaten voting, Penn mollifier layers for inverse PDEs, Medtronic Hugo surgical robot FDA clearance, Alteryx Agent Studio at Inspire 2026, and Specific AI B2B workforce platform.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — May 24, 2026
item.description: 15 stories: Anthropic-Gates Foundation 200M AI-for-good partnership, SAP acquires Prior Labs for 1B, GitHub Copilot usage-based billing, Google Lyria 3 Pro music expansion, Zyphra Zaya1 8B reasoning for AMD, Telegram bot-to-bot AI agents, Cursor Composer 2.5, Amazon Alexa AI podcasts, Bird AI breast cancer ultrasound, Penn light-matter chip for efficient AI, neural network noise calibration, Colorado AI accountability act, Trump admin AI oversight reversal, Take It Down Act FTC enforcement, and Runware 50M inference platform raise.

item.title: The Vault AI Edition — May 23, 2026
item.description: 14 stories: OpenAI model disproves 80-year Erdős geometry conjecture, Walmart Sparky AI lifts order value 35 percent, GitHub TeamPCP redefines pair programming, Anthropic Ramp agentic finance suite, Baidu ERNIE 5.5 challenges GPT-5, DeepMind AlphaEvolve redesigns TPU layout, EU Parliament votes to ban real-time facial recognition, India Personal Data Protection Board begins enforcement, NIST post-quantum FIPS standards take effect, Nvidia healthcare AI ROI survey shows 4.2x returns, and more.
